recent_excerpts: Check Point Research found a bug in VECT ransomware's encryption logic that permanently destroys files on Windows systems — no recovery possible even after paying.
The Kyber ransomware gang is using a variant that implements Kyber1024 post-quantum encryption to target Windows and VMware ESXi systems, according to a BleepingComputer analysis.
Kyber ransomware deploys two distinct payloads to encrypt both Windows systems and VMware ESXi servers, using a custom tool to wipe ESXi snapshots and hinder recovery.
